steps.title: Isolate the y-term
steps.content: Subtract $\tfrac{1}{5xy}$ from both sides and combine over the common denominator $20xy^{2}z$: $$xyz=\tfrac{5x-4z}{20xy^{2}z}.$$

steps.title: Solve for y
steps.content: Multiply by $y$ and divide by $xz$: $y^{2}=\tfrac{5x-4z}{20x^{2}z^{2}}$, so $y=\sqrt{\tfrac{5x-4z}{20x^{2}z^{2}}}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
